# ReactBox
> ReactBox is a type-safe polymorphic React box component library with flexible rendering and smart defaults. It provides a powerful foundation for building reusable UI components with TypeScript support, automatic prop forwarding, and component composition patterns.

All components are written in TypeScript and designed for modern React applications. The library focuses on providing a simple yet powerful primitive for building UI components with full type safety and excellent developer experience.

**Important notes for agents:**
- ReactBox is a lightweight component library focused on the primitive box component pattern
- All components are fully typed with TypeScript and support polymorphic `as` prop
- The `withProps` static method allows creating pre-configured component variants
- Zero runtime overhead - just a thin wrapper around React's forwardRef
- Works seamlessly with Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, and any styling solution
- Peer dependencies: React, React DOM, and classnames

## Documentation
- [Homepage](https://js.work): Project homepage with documentation
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/afeiship/react-box): Source code and issues
- [NPM Package](https://www.npmjs.org/package/@jswork/react-box): Package information and versions
- [Preview/Demo](https://afeiship.github.io/react-box/): Live preview and examples

## Key Features
- **Type-Safe Polymorphic Component**: Render any HTML element with full TypeScript support
- **Smart Prop Forwarding**: Automatically forwards props to the underlying element
- **Static withProps Method**: Create pre-configured component variants with default props
- **ForwardRef Support**: Full ref forwarding for DOM access and integration
- **Zero Runtime Cost**: Minimal abstraction layer with no performance penalty
- **Framework Agnostic**: Works with Next.js, Remix, Vite, and any React setup
- **Tailwind CSS Friendly**: Designed to work perfectly with utility-first CSS

## Installation

### Using npm
```bash
npm install @jswork/react-box
```

### Using yarn
```bash
yarn add @jswork/react-box
```

### Using pnpm
```bash
pnpm add @jswork/react-box
```

## Usage

### Basic Usage
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

function App() {
  return (
    <ReactBox className="p-4 bg-white rounded shadow">
      Hello, ReactBox!
    </ReactBox>
  );
}
```

### Polymorphic Usage
Render different HTML elements using the `as` prop:

```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

// Render as a button
<ReactBox as="button" className="btn btn-primary">
  Click me
</ReactBox>

// Render as a link
<ReactBox as="a" href="/about" className="nav-link">
  About
</ReactBox>

// Render as a section
<ReactBox as="section" className="hero-section">
  Hero Content
</ReactBox>
```

### Using withProps for Component Variants
Create pre-configured components with default props:

```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

// Create a Card component with default styles
const Card = ReactBox.withProps({
  className: 'p-6 bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md',
  as: 'article'
});

// Create a Button component
const Button = ReactBox.withProps({
  className: 'px-4 py-2 bg-blue-500 text-white rounded hover:bg-blue-600',
  as: 'button',
  type: 'button'
});

// Use your custom components
function App() {
  return (
    <>
      Card>
        <h2>Card Title</h2>
        <p>Card content goes here...</p>
      </Card>
      <Button>Click me</Button>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Type-Safe Component Composition
```tsx
import { ReactBox, type ReactBoxProps } from '@jswork/react-box';

type FlexProps = ReactBoxProps<'div'> & {
  direction?: 'row' | 'column';
  align?: 'start' | 'center' | 'end';
  justify?: 'start' | 'center' | 'end' | 'between';
};

function Flex({ direction = 'row', align = 'start', justify = 'start', className, ...props }: FlexProps) {
  const classes = [
    'flex',
    direction === 'row' ? 'flex-row' : 'flex-col',
    `align-${align}`,
    `justify-${justify}`,
    className
  ].filter(Boolean).join(' ');

  return <ReactBox className={classes} {...props} />;
}
```

### Styling with Tailwind CSS
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

function StyledComponents() {
  return (
    <>
      {/* Container with spacing */}
      <ReactBox className="container mx-auto px-4">
        Content
      </ReactBox>

      {/* Flex layout */}
      <ReactBox className="flex items-center justify-between gap-4">
        <div>Left</div>
        <div>Right</div>
      </ReactBox>

      {/* Grid layout */}
      <ReactBox className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-6">
        <div>Item 1</div>
        <div>Item 2</div>
        <div>Item 3</div>
      </ReactBox>
    </>
  );
}
```

## API Reference

### ReactBox
The main polymorphic box component.

#### Props
- `as?: ElementType` - The HTML element or React component to render (default: `'div'`)
- `className?: string` - CSS class names
- `ref?: Ref` - React ref forwarded to the underlying element
- All other HTML attributes of the specified element type are supported

#### Type Definition
```typescript
type ReactBoxProps<C extends ElementType> = Props<C>;

type ReactBoxComponent = (<C extends ElementType = 'div'>(
  props: Props<C> & { ref?: React.Ref<React.ComponentRef<C>> }
) => React.ReactElement) & {
  displayName?: string;
  withProps<D extends Record<string, unknown>>(
    defaultProps: D
  ): WithPropsReturnType<D>;
};
```

### withProps
Static method to create a new component with merged default props.

```typescript
const CustomComponent = ReactBox.withProps({
  as: 'button',
  className: 'px-4 py-2 bg-blue-500',
  type: 'button'
});
```

## TypeScript Support

ReactBox is built with TypeScript from the ground up and provides excellent type inference:

```typescript
import { ReactBox, type ReactBoxProps } from '@jswork/react-box';

// Type-safe props for button element
type ButtonProps = ReactBoxProps<'button'> & {
  variant?: 'primary' | 'secondary';
};

function Button({ variant = 'primary', ...props }: ButtonProps) {
  const variantClass = variant === 'primary'
    ? 'bg-blue-500 hover:bg-blue-600'
    : 'bg-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-600';

  return (
    <ReactBox
      as="button"
      className={`px-4 py-2 rounded ${variantClass}`}
      {...props}
    />
  );
}
```

## Usage Notes for Agents

- **Default Element**: ReactBox renders as a `div` by default, but this can be changed with the `as` prop
- **Type Safety**: When using the `as` prop, TypeScript will infer the correct props for that element
- **Ref Forwarding**: Refs are automatically forwarded to the underlying element
- **ClassName Merging**: Use your preferred className utility (clsx, cn, etc.) for merging classes
- **Styling**: ReactBox is styling-agnostic and works with any CSS solution
- **Component Variants**: Use `withProps` to create reusable component variants with default props
- **Dependencies**: Ensure `classnames` peer dependency is installed in your project

## Development
- **Source**: [packages/lib/src](https://github.com/afeiship/react-box/tree/main/packages/lib/src)
- **Built with**: TypeScript, React, tsup
- **License**: MIT

## Examples

### Create a Layout System
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

const Container = ReactBox.withProps({
  className: 'container mx-auto px-4'
});

const Row = ReactBox.withProps({
  className: 'flex flex-row gap-4'
});

const Col = ReactBox.withProps({
  className: 'flex-1'
});

// Usage
function Layout() {
  return (
    <Container>
      <Row>
        <Col>Column 1</Col>
        <Col>Column 2</Col>
        <Col>Column 3</Col>
      </Row>
    </Container>
  );
}
```

### Create a Design System
```tsx
import { ReactBox } from '@jswork/react-box';

// Base components
const Box = ReactBox;

const Text = ReactBox.withProps({
  as: 'p',
  className: 'text-gray-700 leading-relaxed'
});

const Heading = ReactBox.withProps({
  as: 'h2',
  className: 'text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-4'
});

// Usage
function Article() {
  return (
    <Box className="article max-w-2xl mx-auto py-8">
      <Heading>Article Title</Heading>
      <Text>
        Article content goes here...
      </Text>
    </Box>
  );
}
```

## Related Packages
- [@jswork/with-props](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@jswork/with-props): Utility for creating components with default props
